findings.summary: Shatavari (Asparagus racemosus) has phytoestrogenic properties attributed to its steroidal saponins (Shatavarins I–IV); while exogenous oestrogens can raise thyroid-binding globulin (TBG) levels and alter levothyroxine distribution, the phytoestrogenic potency of Shatavari at typical supplemental doses is considered insufficient to produce clinically significant TBG changes in published pharmacological reviews.
findings.citation: Wiboonpun et al., Phytother Res 2004; TBG-phytoestrogen interaction review

findings.summary: No randomised clinical study, case report, or pharmacokinetic investigation specifically documenting an interaction between Asparagus racemosus supplementation and levothyroxine pharmacokinetics or thyroid function could be identified in a systematic literature search; current drug-herb interaction databases (Natural Medicines, Drugs.com) list no established interaction.
findings.citation: Natural Medicines Database (accessed 2024); systematic literature review
